pi_items.source: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 standard country codes verified against https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2. DE=Germany, FR=France, IT=Italy

ISO 4217 currency code verified against https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217. EUR is the official code for Euro, which is the sole currency of Germany, France, and Italy

WooCommerce currency position setting verified against https://woocommerce.com/document/shop-currency/ and WEBAPPICK guide https://webappick.com/how-to-change-currency-in-woocommerce/. The four WooCommerce position options are: left, right, left_space, and right_space. European convention places currency symbol to the right with space (e.g., '100.00 €')

European number formatting standard verified against https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decimal_separator and https://data.europa.eu/apps/data-visualisation-guide/number-formatting. The dot (period) is the standard thousands separator in Germany, France, and Italy (e.g., 1.234.567,89)

European number formatting standard verified against https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decimal_separator and Microsoft Learn https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/globalization/locale/number-formatting. The comma is the standard decimal separator in Germany, France, and Italy (e.g., 1.234.567,89)

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring a WooCommerce store for European expansion to Germany (DE), France (FR), and Italy (IT). All metadata claims are verified: the country codes are valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes; EUR is the correct ISO 4217 code for the Euro currency used in all three countries; right_space is a valid WooCommerce currency position placing the symbol to the right with space (conforming to European convention); the dot (.) as thousands separator and comma (,) as decimal separator are standard European number formatting conventions used in Germany, France, and Italy.
